rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component thioether-tetrazole synthesis from TMSN3, vinyl arenes, and arylthiols, proceeding through a thiyl radical addition / radical-polar crossover pathway as probed by a radical clock experiment.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates a thiyl radical from the arylthiol (ArSH); the thiyl radical adds to the alkene, and subsequent oxidation/azide trapping with the tetrazole-forming step closes the cycle.

key_intermediates.name: cyclopropylcarbinyl radical from thiyl addition to 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinylbenzene
benzylic cation (after oxidation of ring-opened radical)

electron_flow: ArSH is oxidized (anodically, mediated by Ar3N) to an arylthiyl radical that adds to the vinylarene; the resulting cyclopropylcarbinyl radical undergoes ring-opening (radical clock), and the ring-opened C-radical is further oxidized to a benzylic cation that is trapped by azide/tetrazole nitrogen to give 32.

probe_reagents.name: 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinylbenzene (cyclopropyl radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 32 isolated in 20% yield (7.6:1 dr), confirming a carbon-radical intermediate that opens the cyclopropane after thiyl addition.

notes: Only the radical clock experiment is shown on this page; other mechanistic probes (if any) are not included here. The ring-opened olefinic thioether-tetrazole product supports a stepwise radical addition followed by radical-polar crossover rather than a concerted pathway.
